patch.value: Dogfood 2026-05-20 low-severity CLI polish backlog (config UX, init verbosity, help alias bloat, default-safety, doc/validator drift)

Low-severity polish items from the 2026-05-20 dogfood pass, grouped to avoid item sprawl. M5: pm config set <key> <value> positional form errors ('too many arguments') and config get/read wording drifts from the real get/set/list/export actions + typed --policy/--format/--criterion flags; align help wording and detect the positional-value mistake. L1: pm init dumps the full settings tree (~193 lines) by default; consider concise default + tree behind --verbose (a --quiet exists). L4: pm create --help (~107 lines) lists --foo-bar and --foo_bar aliases separately; collapse alias display. L5: create changed_fields lists every field including unset body (noise on create). L6: cal --help lists --include deadlines|reminders|events|all but validator also accepts 'scheduled' (doc/validator drift). L7: pm guide (package uninstalled) prints 'Unknown command' with no install hint; package-catalog referenced in maintainer memory is not a command. L8: --priority 9 vs --priority high give slightly different phrasings. L9: templates package installs but pm templates shows count:0 (no starter templates ship). L10: pm gc deletes by default (dry_run:false) while normalize/update-many default to dry-run (inconsistent default safety); pm delete has no --force/dry-run. L11: --event start=+3d,end=+3d rejects equal timestamps without hinting equal==invalid. Also H1 note: pm validate already supports --strict-exit/--fail-on-warn (exits non-zero on ok:false) — default exit 0 is correct because ok:false includes advisory metadata-completeness warnings; improve discoverability in docs/help rather than changing the default.

Each listed sub-item is either fixed with a regression test or explicitly declined with rationale; no change drops coverage below 100%; doc/validator drift (L6) resolved; default-safety inconsistencies (L10) reviewed.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Resuming the remaining low-severity polish backlog: L1 (concise pm init), L4 (collapse help underscore-aliases), L7 (install hint for known-but-uninstalled package commands), L8 (named priority aliases), L9 (ship starter templates), L10 (default-safety: pm delete --dry-run + gc help clarity). Coordinating two specialized sub-agents under one PR; tracking via Plan.

value.author: help-output-subagent
value.text: help-output-subagent START: L1 (concise pm init default + --verbose full tree) and L4 (hide pure snake_case underscore-duplicate aliases from --help while keeping them parse-functional). Plan: L4 uses commander new Option(flags,desc).hideHelp() in register-mutation.ts registerCommanderOptionContracts for aliasOptions whose long == kebab-canonical with - replaced by _; also hide hand-written snake aliases in register-mutation (allow_audit_update etc.), update-many block, plan block, schema block, and register-list-query (--assignee_filter, --include_unparented). KEEP semantic aliases visible (--fail-on-warn, --local, --active-only, --ac, --rank, --estimate). L1 adds --verbose to init and gates the full settings tree behind it. JSON help payload unchanged (reads command.options which hideHelp keeps). Running focused specs next.

value.author: help-output-subagent
value.text:
help-output-subagent DONE (L1+L4 both complete).

L4 (snake-alias help bloat): RULE = hide an alias only when its long flag is the underscore form of an existing kebab long flag (normalize both flags by replacing interior hyphens with underscores; equal + non-identical => hidden). Multi-long-flag specs are matched against EVERY long flag (so --estimated_minutes hides under '--estimate, --estimated-minutes'). Semantic/word aliases stay visible (--ac, --rank, --step, --decision, --discovery, --validation, --fail-on-warn, --local, --active-only). Mechanism: commander new Option(flags,desc).hideHelp() (repeatable ones get .argParser(collect)); option stays in command.options so JSON help payload + completion are unchanged, only text --help omits it; parse-time aliases still populate their option keys. New pure-logic helper src/core/shared/option-alias-visibility.ts (isPureSnakeCaseAlias/extractLongFlag/extractLongFlags) added to vitest include at 100% coverage.

L1 (concise pm init): added --verbose flag; default toon output now a concise summary (path, id_prefix, governance_preset, telemetry.enabled+capture_level, output_format, created_dirs+count, ALL warnings incl already_exists:, wizard_used, installed_packages, next_steps, agent_guidance, hint). Full settings tree only with --verbose OR --json (JSON shape unchanged for consumers). summarizeInitResult() in init.ts. Measured: pm init 193 -> 60 lines default; --verbose still 193; --json unchanged (settings present).

Files changed: src/cli/commands/init.ts (summarizeInitResult + InitConciseResult), src/cli/commands/index.ts (export), src/cli/register-setup.ts (init --verbose + gate), src/cli/register-mutation.ts (addHiddenOption + hide create/update via contracts + update-many/plan/schema/update hand-written snake aliases), src/cli/register-list-query.ts (hide --assignee_filter on list/aggregate/context, --include_unparented on aggregate), src/core/shared/option-alias-visibility.ts (NEW helper), vitest.config.ts (include helper). Tests: tests/unit/option-alias-visibility.spec.ts (NEW), tests/unit/init-command.spec.ts (+summary tests), tests/integration/help-runtime.spec.ts (+init concise/verbose + alias-hiding regression).

Tests run (all PASS): option-alias-visibility 16; init-command 8; help-runtime 27; completion 212-total batch (completion/contracts/create/update/task-lifecycle); update-many/list/schema/close 43; cli.integration+governance 78; structure-exports+bootstrap-args 39. tsc --noEmit clean. Helper coverage 100% stmts/branch/funcs/lines.

COORDINATOR SHARED-FILE TODOs (I did NOT touch these - all forbidden in my scope):
1. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]: add 'Concise pm init default output (full settings tree now behind --verbose; --json unchanged)' and 'Hidden pure snake_case underscore-duplicate option aliases from --help across mutation/list commands (still functional as parse-time aliases)'.
2. docs/COMMANDS + docs/AGENT_GUIDE: optionally document pm init --verbose and that snake aliases are hidden-but-functional.
3. completion.ts (bash/zsh/fish hand-maintained): snake aliases still listed there - functionally harmless (still parse) but for consistency the coordinator may prune --foo_bar completion entries. Not required.
4. SDK contracts (src/sdk/cli-contracts*): NO change needed - I hid via registration only, contract data untouched; existing help-runtime JSON assertion (--blocked_by absent / --blocked-by aliases include --blocked_by) still passes.

value.author: cli-input-subagent
value.text: START [cli-input-ergonomics sub-agent] Beginning L8/L7/L9 from pm-5k2w. Plan: L8 shared priority resolver in src/core/item/priority.ts (mapping critical=0,high=1,medium=2,low=3,minimal=4; numeric 0..4 still valid; bad input throws USAGE listing both forms), called from create.ts ensurePriority + update.ts ensurePriority. L7 add concrete command->package install hint in error-guidance.ts unknown_command block (guide->@unbrained/pm-guide-shell via 'pm install guide-shell'; templates->@unbrained/pm-templates via 'pm install templates'; calendar/cal->@unbrained/pm-calendar via 'pm install calendar'); keep existing fields. L9 ship BUILTIN starter templates (bug/feature/spike/chore) in packages/pm-templates/extensions/templates/runtime.ts so 'pm templates' returns count>0 out of the box; user-saved templates override builtins; mirror into runtime.js. Not touching files owned by the concurrent agent/coordinator.

value.author: cli-input-subagent
value.text:
DONE [cli-input-ergonomics sub-agent] L8/L7/L9 all complete; tsc (main+packages) clean; focused specs pass.

L8 (named priority aliases): shared resolver src/core/item/priority.ts -> resolvePriority(raw): Priority. Mapping critical=0, high=1, medium=2, low=3, minimal=4 (case-insensitive, trims ws). Numeric 0..4 unchanged (strict /^[0-4]$/ after trim; rejects 1.0/5/-1). Bad/empty input throws PmCliError USAGE: 'Invalid priority "X". Accepted values: numbers 0..4 (0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium, 3=low, 4=minimal) or names (critical, high, medium, low, minimal), case-insensitive.' Also exports PRIORITY_NAME_TO_VALUE + PRIORITY_ACCEPTED_FORMS_HINT. Wired from create.ts ensurePriority() and update.ts ensurePriority() (both now just delegate to resolvePriority). Added priority.ts to vitest include; 100% cover (13/13 stmts, 6/6 branch, 2/2 func).

L7 (install hint for known package commands): added KNOWN_PACKAGE_COMMAND_HINTS catalog + resolveKnownPackageCommandHint() in src/cli/error-guidance.ts; emits concrete hint in the unknown_command branch of buildCommanderErrorGuidance (~line 682). Catalog: guide->@unbrained/pm-guide-shell ('pm install guide-shell'); templates->@unbrained/pm-templates ('pm install templates'); calendar & cal->@unbrained/pm-calendar ('pm install calendar'). Keeps all existing fields; install command added to examples (deduped, first) + next_steps (first), and package name woven into title/required/why. Non-package unknown commands unchanged. Note: a generic optional-package hint already lived in src/cli/commander-usage.ts (FORBIDDEN file, not touched) so the generic line still trails; my concrete hint leads. Added dedupeStrings() helper.

L9 (builtin starter templates): implemented in packages/pm-templates/extensions/templates/runtime.ts (+ mirrored runtime.js). BUILTIN_TEMPLATES = bug (type=Issue, priority=1, acceptance/expected/actual + repro body), feature (type=Feature, priority=2, whyNow + goal body), spike (type=Task, priority=2, estimatedMinutes=120, timeboxed body), chore (type=Chore, priority=3). Option keys are camelCase create keys so 'pm create --template <name>' merges them directly. runTemplatesList now merges user+builtin (user shadows same-name builtin) and returns {templates,count,builtin_templates,user_templates}. runTemplatesShow returns {name,source:'builtin'|'user',...,path}; builtins use path 'builtin:<name>' and ts 1970-01-01. New resolveTemplateDocument()/readUserTemplateNames()/builtinTemplateDocument(); save still preserves user created_at via the original readStoredTemplateDocument. NOTE: core SDK fallback src/cli/commands/templates.ts (used only when package not installed) was NOT changed — its loadCreateTemplateOptions/runTemplatesList have no builtins. Since 'pm templates' is package-provided (unknown without the package, see L7), builtins only need to live in the package runtime. Flagging in case the coordinator wants parity in the SDK fallback.

FILES CHANGED (mine):
- NEW src/core/item/priority.ts
- src/cli/commands/create.ts (import + ensurePriority delegates)
- src/cli/commands/update.ts (import + ensurePriority delegates)
- src/cli/error-guidance.ts (catalog + resolver + dedupeStrings + unknown_command hint)
- packages/pm-templates/extensions/templates/runtime.ts AND runtime.js (builtins; behavior-identical mirrors)
- vitest.config.ts (added src/core/item/priority.ts to include only)
- NEW tests/unit/priority.spec.ts
- tests/unit/error-guidance.spec.ts (+2 cases: install-hint, no-hint-for-unknown)
- tests/unit/templates-command.spec.ts (+3 cases: builtins list, create-from-builtin, user-override)

TESTS (PM_RUN_TESTS_SKIP_BUILD=1, --reporter=dot): priority 8 PASS; error-guidance 9 PASS; templates-command 9 PASS (6 old + 3 new); create-command + update-command + update-many 112 PASS; completion + contracts + cli.integration 180 PASS. priority.ts coverage 100%. tsc --noEmit (tsconfig.json) clean; tsc -p tsconfig.packages.json clean.

MANUAL VERIFY (temp PM_PATH, minimal preset): create --priority high=>1, --priority 2=>2, Critical=>0, MINIMAL=>4, bogus=>USAGE error listing both forms; update --priority high=>1, badword=>same error. pm guide (uninstalled) => title/required/why name @unbrained/pm-guide-shell, examples+next_steps include 'pm install guide-shell'; pm cal => @unbrained/pm-calendar / 'pm install calendar'; pm templates (uninstalled) => @unbrained/pm-templates / 'pm install templates'. After 'pm install templates --project': pm templates --json => count 4 (bug,chore,feature,spike); show spike => source:builtin; create --template bug (no save) => Issue/priority 1; saving user 'bug' then show => source:user/type=Task, list shows bug once (user_templates), builtin_templates drops bug.

COORDINATOR SHARED-FILE TODOs (I did NOT touch these):
1. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]: add (a) '--priority now accepts named levels (critical/high/medium/low/minimal, case-insensitive) in create and update; numeric 0..4 still works'; (b) 'Unknown package-provided commands (guide/templates/calendar/cal) now show a concrete install command'; (c) 'pm templates ships builtin starter templates (bug/feature/spike/chore) so the catalog is non-empty out of the box; user templates override builtins'.
2. docs/COMMANDS + docs/AGENT_GUIDE: document named priority aliases; document builtin templates + that pm templates show now returns a source field and list returns builtin_templates/user_templates.
3. completion.ts (bash/zsh/fish): optional — could add builtin template names (bug/feature/spike/chore) to 'templates show' value completion; not required.
4. SDK contracts (src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts): no change strictly needed (priority option still a string; resolver runs at execution). If contract docs describe priority as 'numeric 0..4', update the description to mention named aliases. templates-show/list result schema (if documented in contracts) now has extra fields (source/builtin_templates/user_templates) — additive.
5. Consider whether src/cli/commands/templates.ts (SDK fallback) should also ship builtins for parity (currently only the package runtime has them).

value.author: config-ux-subagent
value.text: config-ux-subagent START: FIX A — add optional [value] positional to config cmd (register-setup.ts) + new pure helper src/core/config/positional-value.ts (resolveConfigPositionalValue) routing key→typed flag (format/policy/criterion) with off/on/true/false synonyms; wire into runConfig only when typed flag not already passed, conflict=error, context=hint. FIX B — shrink invalid-key error to canonical kebab forms + '(underscore variants also accepted)'. New unit spec at 100% cov, add helper to vitest include. Verify tsc + tests + smoke.

value.author: config-ux-subagent
value.text:
config-ux-subagent DONE (FIX A + FIX B both complete; item left open per instructions).

FIX A (positional value for `pm config set <key> <value>`):
- NEW pure helper src/core/config/positional-value.ts exporting resolveConfigPositionalValue(keyOrAlias, value) -> discriminated union. Accepts BOTH kebab and snake key forms (normalizes interior '-' to '_'). Mapping table:
  * item-format -> {flag:'format', value} (verbatim)
  * definition-of-done, metadata-required-fields, lifecycle-stale-blocker-reason-patterns, lifecycle-closure-like-blocked-reason-patterns, lifecycle-closure-like-resolution-patterns, lifecycle-closure-like-actual-result-patterns -> {flag:'criterion', values:[value]}
  * history-missing-stream-policy, sprint-release-format-policy, parent-reference-policy, metadata-validation-profile, governance-preset, governance-ownership-enforcement, governance-create-mode-default, governance-close-validation-default, governance-parent-reference-policy, governance-metadata-validation-profile, governance-force-required-for-stale-lock, test-result-tracking, telemetry-tracking -> {flag:'policy', value}. For the three enabled/disabled-valued keys (governance-force-required-for-stale-lock, test-result-tracking, telemetry-tracking) the synonyms off->disabled, on->enabled, true->enabled, false->disabled (case-insensitive) are applied; all other policy keys pass the value through unchanged so existing validators keep their good error messages.
  * context -> {routable:false, reason: hint naming --default-depth/--activity-limit/--stale-threshold-days/--section-<name>}
  * unknown key -> {routable:false} (caller hits the FIX-B invalid-key error first since normalizeKey runs before routing)
- Wired in src/cli/commands/config.ts: new optional 6th param valueValue on runConfig; private applyPositionalValue(action,key,value,options) injects the routed flag into options ONLY when (a) action==='set', (b) key present, (c) the typed flag was NOT already supplied. If the explicit flag matches the positional -> no-op; if it conflicts -> clear USAGE error; positional on non-set action or with no key -> clear USAGE error; context -> hint error. All existing typed-flag behavior 100% intact (back-compat verified).
- register-setup.ts: added 4th positional [value] argument + help text; action handler now resolves value with shorthand-scope shift (config set <key> <value> => action=set,key,value; config project set <key> <value> also works) and passes resolvedValue as 6th arg.
- MCP parity: src/mcp/server.ts config case now passes readString(args/options,'value') as 6th arg; src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts config schema-contract optional list gains 'value' (param 'value' already typed in props map).

FIX B (token-heavy invalid-key error): src/cli/commands/config.ts normalizeKey now lists CANONICAL_CONFIG_KEYS (21 kebab forms derived from CONFIG_KEY_ALIASES[k][0]) + '(underscore variants also accepted)' instead of all ~45 kebab+snake CONFIG_KEY_VALUES. Snake forms still fully accepted as input. No other code/tests depended on the old exact string (only assertion was exitCode===USAGE).

FILES CHANGED:
- NEW src/core/config/positional-value.ts (in vitest include @100% cover)
- src/cli/commands/config.ts (import, CANONICAL_CONFIG_KEYS, applyPositionalValue, runConfig 6th param, shortened error)
- src/cli/register-setup.ts ([value] arg + handler wiring + shorthand shift)
- src/mcp/server.ts (config value pass-through)
- src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts (config optional += 'value')
- vitest.config.ts (added positional-value.ts to coverage include only; NOT exclude)
- docs/CONFIGURATION.md (documented positional value form + context-uses-flags note)
- NEW tests/unit/config-positional-value.spec.ts (8 tests, helper @100%)
- tests/unit/config-command.spec.ts (+10 wiring tests)

TESTS (all PASS): config-positional-value 8; config-command 36 (was 26, +10); helper coverage 100% stmts(22/22)/branch(18/18)/funcs(3/3)/lines. Broader: help-runtime 27 + completion 212-batch (163 across 4 files); release-readiness-runtime 32 (coverage-alignment + config gate green); contracts-command + mcp-dynamic-package-actions + claude-plugin-contract 34; structure-exports + bootstrap-args + cli.integration 114. tsc --noEmit (tsconfig.json) clean. docs-skills gate PASS. (packages untouched, tsconfig.packages.json not needed.)

MANUAL SMOKE (pnpm build once; /tmp/cfgprobe, init --defaults): `config set telemetry-tracking off`->policy disabled changed:true; `... disabled`->changed:false; `... item-format toon`->format toon; `... governance-preset strict`->policy strict changed:true; `... definition-of-done "Tests pass"`->criteria ["Tests pass"]. `config set bogus-key foo`-> shortened 21-key error + underscore note. `config set context deep`-> not-routable hint (flags listed; error layer even auto-added suggested_retry). `config set telemetry-tracking off --policy enabled`-> conflict USAGE error. Back-compat `config set telemetry-tracking --policy enabled` and `config project set telemetry-tracking on` both work.

COORDINATOR TODOs (shared files I did NOT touch):
1. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] (forbidden for me): add
   - 'pm config set <key> <value> now accepts the value as a positional argument, routed to the correct typed flag (--policy/--format/--criterion) by key; off/on/true/false map to disabled/enabled for boolean policy keys.'
   - 'Shortened the invalid config-key error to list canonical kebab keys only (underscore variants still accepted).'
2. docs: COMMANDS.md/AGENT_GUIDE.md were left as-is (no existing `config set` detail there; CONFIGURATION.md is the canonical home and was updated). Optional to cross-link.
3. completion.ts NOT changed (config values are not value-completed there; positional is free-form). No action required.
